topicArea: Philosophy of Friendship, Its Classical Taxonomy & the Quantified Circle

questions.passage:
The word has become a number. A profile announces that a man has one thousand four hundred friends, and the figure is offered without irony by an interface that has also made the word a verb, something one does with a click, and a commodity, something a platform can promise to help you make more of. It would be easy to declare this a corruption and mourn. It is more useful to ask what exactly is being counted, because the question turns out to be ancient, and the oldest taxonomy of friendship remains the sharpest instrument for taking the inventory.

That taxonomy distinguished three grounds on which one person befriends another: utility, pleasure, and character. Friends of utility value each other for benefit exchanged, the partner, the colleague, the neighbor with the ladder; friends of pleasure for the enjoyment of company, the wit, the companion of the game and the table. Both are genuine forms, and both are structurally fragile, since they dissolve when the benefit or the pleasure stops, being attached to what the friend provides rather than to who the friend is. The third form takes the friend's character as its object: each wishes the other's good for the other's own sake, and each, valuing what the other is rather than what the other supplies, has reason to remain when supplying stops. Such friendship, the tradition held, is necessarily rare, since it requires time, shared life, and mutual testing; one cannot have many friends of this kind, not because of scheduling, but because its conditions cannot be mass-produced.

The taxonomy's first modern service is diagnostic. Much of what present vocabulary calls friendship is friendship of utility wearing a warmer name, and an entire professional idiom, networking, connections, maintaining relationships, has grown up to manage such ties while borrowing the prestige of the older word. The borrowing matters. A person with hundreds of connections and no one to call from the hospital has not miscounted; he has misfiled, and the vocabulary helped him do it, since it prices all ties in a single currency and thereby hides the difference between an alliance and an attachment.

But the taxonomy can also be wielded snobbishly, and its modern users often inherit the snobbery with the insight. The tradition ranked character friendship as alone complete and tended to treat the other forms as deficient copies, a ranking easiest to endorse from a position of leisure. Character friendship as classically described, long cultivation, shared contemplation, abundant time, was in practice a luxury of those whose material lives were secured, frequently by the labor of people too busy for cultivated mutual contemplation. The mutual aid of the overworked, meals delivered, children watched, wages lent, may be filed under utility, but the filing insults it: such ties routinely carry loyalty, affection, and sacrifice that the taxonomy's upper shelf would be lucky to match. A classification that grades friendship by its leisure content has confused the container with the contents.

What the tradition saw truly, beneath its rankings, is that friendship of the fullest kind does distinctive work that no other relation performs. The close friend is a mirror with judgment: someone who knows the self's history well enough to recognize its patterns, cares enough to attend, and stands far enough outside to say what the self cannot see. Flattery is available everywhere; the friend's frankness, licensed by proven goodwill, is nearly the only correction most adults will accept. This work requires partiality, the friend is on your side, and the partiality sits awkwardly with moralities of strict impartial concern, which is not an argument against friendship but a datum about morality: a theory with no room for loyalty has misdescribed the creatures it legislates for.

Against this account, the quantified circle looks less like corruption than like compression. The platform files every tie, the ladder neighbor, the table companion, the mirror with judgment, under one word, one button, one count, and supports all of them with the same thin instrument: ambient awareness, the passive scroll of others' curated days. Thin ties genuinely benefit, weak links carry news, opportunities, the pleasant hum of a wide acquaintance, and the interface is honest about breadth. What it cannot host is the third form's conditions, time, testing, the unguarded presence in which frankness is possible, and a culture that conducts its friendship mainly there will keep the word while quietly losing the referent's rarest case.

The number, then, was never the scandal. Fourteen hundred is a fine count of something, alliances, acquaintances, audiences, and the man who knows which of them would sit with him in the hospital corridor has lost nothing to the interface. The scandal would be forgetting that the question exists, and the old taxonomy's continuing use is exactly that it keeps the question askable: not how many, which the platform answers instantly, but of what kind, which it cannot answer at all.
questions.stem: Which of the following best expresses the main idea of the passage?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The classical taxonomy, corrected for its snobbery, remains the sharpest instrument for sorting ties that quantified circles compress into one count.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Online platforms have destroyed friendship by replacing genuine ties with meaningless counts that flatter users while quietly isolating them.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Friendships of utility and pleasure are deficient copies of character friendship and should be abandoned by anyone seeking complete relationships.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Because character friendship was historically a luxury of the leisured, the classical taxonomy has nothing to teach societies committed to equality.

explanations.A: Correct. The passage deploys the three-ground taxonomy diagnostically, exposes the networking idiom's misfiling, corrects the tradition's leisure-class snobbery while preserving its core insight about the mirror with judgment, and concludes that platforms compress all kinds into one count, honest about breadth but unable to host the third form's conditions, leaving the taxonomy's question, of what kind, as the essential one.
explanations.B: This choice declares the corruption the first paragraph sets aside as the easy response and the final paragraph explicitly denies: the number was never the scandal, the man who knows which ties would sit with him in the hospital has lost nothing to the interface, and thin ties are credited with genuine benefits.
explanations.C: This choice repeats the ranking the fourth paragraph criticizes as snobbery. The passage insists utility and pleasure friendships are genuine forms, and argues that the mutual aid of the overworked carries loyalty and sacrifice the upper shelf would be lucky to match, so abandonment of the lesser forms is the opposite of its counsel.
explanations.D: This choice takes the fourth paragraph's correction as a refutation. The passage criticizes the classical account's leisure bias precisely in order to keep using the taxonomy, what the tradition saw truly beneath its rankings, so the class critique qualifies the instrument rather than retiring it.

questions.stem: The primary function of the fourth paragraph is to:

choices.label: A
choices.text: argue that character friendship is impossible for anyone who must work for a living.

choices.label: B
choices.text: explain why platform interfaces, built for breadth and ambient awareness, cannot support the demanding conditions of character friendship.

choices.label: C
choices.text: defend the taxonomy against the charge of vagueness by refining its definitions.

choices.label: D
choices.text: correct the taxonomy's inherited snobbery by showing its ranking reflected leisure and that utility ties can carry real devotion.

explanations.A: This choice hardens the paragraph's historical observation into an impossibility claim. The point is that character friendship as classically described presupposed secured leisure, a critique of the description and its ranking, not a decree that working people cannot form the deepest ties; the paragraph's own examples of working-class devotion cut the other way.
explanations.B: This choice belongs to the sixth paragraph, where the interface's thin instrument, ambient awareness, is shown unable to host time, testing, and unguarded frankness. The fourth paragraph is occupied with the taxonomy's class bias, not with platforms.
explanations.C: This choice invents a vagueness charge no one in the passage raises. The paragraph's target is the taxonomy's evaluative ranking, its treatment of utility and pleasure as deficient copies, not the precision of its categories, which the passage continues to use as stated.
explanations.D: Correct. The paragraph warns that modern users inherit the snobbery with the insight, traces the ranking to positions of leisure secured by others' labor, and argues that the mutual aid of the overworked, meals delivered, wages lent, is insulted by mere utility filing, since such ties carry loyalty, affection, and sacrifice the taxonomy's upper shelf would be lucky to match.

questions.stem: According to the second paragraph, friendships of utility and pleasure are structurally fragile because:

choices.label: A
choices.text: they typically involve people of unequal social standing.

choices.label: B
choices.text: modern life provides too little time to maintain them properly.

choices.label: C
choices.text: they attach to what the friend provides rather than to who the friend is, dissolving when the benefit stops.

choices.label: D
choices.text: the parties to such friendships usually conceal their true instrumental motives from each other in order to preserve the warmer name.

explanations.A: This choice imports an inequality the paragraph does not mention. The fragility is located in the tie's object, provision rather than person, and applies equally between social equals; the passage's ladder neighbor and table companion carry no implication of rank.
explanations.B: This choice attributes the fragility to scheduling, which the paragraph explicitly excludes in the adjacent claim about character friendship, whose rarity is not because of scheduling. The lesser forms' instability is structural, following from their ground, not from modern time pressure.
explanations.C: Correct. The paragraph states that both forms are genuine yet structurally fragile, since they dissolve when the benefit or the pleasure stops, being attached to what the friend provides rather than to who the friend is, whereas the third form, valuing what the other is, has reason to remain when supplying stops.
explanations.D: This choice adds deception the taxonomy does not require. Friends of utility can be perfectly candid about the exchange, the colleague, the neighbor with the ladder, and the fragility follows from the tie's ground even under full mutual transparency.

questions.stem: The author's attitude toward the tension between friendship's partiality and moralities of strict impartial concern is best described as:

choices.label: A
choices.text: resolving it in favor of friendship, since a theory with no room for loyalty has misdescribed the creatures it legislates for.

choices.label: B
choices.text: resolving it in favor of impartiality, concluding that the friend's partiality is a moral failing to be outgrown as maturity develops.

choices.label: C
choices.text: treating the tension as illusory, since friendship involves no genuine partiality.

choices.label: D
choices.text: declining to address the tension, since the passage confines itself to descriptive claims.

explanations.A: Correct. The fifth paragraph acknowledges that partiality, the friend is on your side, sits awkwardly with strict impartial moralities, and then rules on the conflict: this is not an argument against friendship but a datum about morality, since a theory with no room for loyalty has misdescribed the creatures it legislates for, placing the burden of revision on the theory.
explanations.B: This choice reverses the paragraph's assignment of fault. The awkward fit is presented as evidence against the moral theory's adequacy, not against friendship's legitimacy; nothing in the passage treats loyalty as a failing awaiting correction.
explanations.C: This choice denies the premise the paragraph affirms. Partiality is asserted as a requirement of the friend's distinctive work, the mirror with judgment stands on your side, so the tension with impartial concern is real, and the passage resolves rather than dissolves it.
explanations.D: This choice mistakes a normative verdict for descriptive restraint. Declaring that a theory has misdescribed the creatures it legislates for is an evaluation of the theory, delivered plainly, not a refusal to engage the question.

questions.stem: A professional maintains warm relations with hundreds of industry contacts, exchanging favors, referrals, and congratulations, and describes this circle as her friends. When she is hospitalized for a month, none visits, though many send messages. Based on the third paragraph, the author would most likely say that she has:

choices.label: A
choices.text: been betrayed by people who were obligated, by the warmth of the relations she maintained with them, to visit her.

choices.label: B
choices.text: misfiled alliances as attachments, aided by a vocabulary that prices all ties in a single currency.

choices.label: C
choices.text: formed friendships of pleasure that failed to mature into friendships of utility.

choices.label: D
choices.text: proven that professional relationships are inherently worthless.

explanations.A: This choice supplies an obligation the taxonomy would not generate. Friends of utility owe what the exchange defines, favors, referrals, and the messages sent arguably discharge it; the disappointment arises from expecting attachment behavior from alliance ties, the misfiling itself, not from any breach.
explanations.B: Correct. The third paragraph describes exactly this case: friendship of utility wearing a warmer name, managed by the networking idiom while borrowing the older word's prestige. The person with hundreds of connections and no one to call from the hospital has not miscounted; he has misfiled, and the vocabulary helped him do it by pricing all ties in one currency and hiding the difference between an alliance and an attachment.
explanations.C: This choice scrambles the taxonomy's categories and their relations. The favor-and-referral circle is utility, not pleasure, and the forms are distinguished by their grounds, not arranged as stages through which ties mature; nothing in the passage describes pleasure ripening into utility.
explanations.D: This choice draws a contempt the passage forbids. Utility friendships are called genuine forms, and thin ties are separately credited with real benefits; the diagnosis is misclassification of kind, not worthlessness of the ties themselves.

questions.stem: Which of the following, if true, would most weaken the sixth paragraph's claim that platform-based interaction cannot host the conditions of character friendship?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Evidence that platform users report enjoying the passive scroll of others' curated days.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Studies documenting exclusively online pairs developing ties with the third form's marks at rates comparable to co-located pairs.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Evidence that weak ties formed online frequently transmit job opportunities and other practical benefits that strong ties rarely carry.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Evidence that some users maintain more than one account on the same platform.

explanations.A: This choice confirms the medium's account of itself. Enjoyment of ambient awareness is fully compatible with, indeed part of, the passage's description of what platforms do well, breadth and hum, and says nothing about whether the third form's conditions can be met there.
explanations.B: Correct. The claim is that the interface's thin instrument cannot host time, testing, and the unguarded presence in which frankness is possible. Documented pairs who never met offline yet developed ties bearing precisely those marks, mutual testing over years, sacrifice, accepted frank correction, at rates matching co-located pairs, would show the conditions being met within the medium, directly contradicting the incapacity claim.
explanations.C: This choice strengthens a concession the paragraph already makes. Weak links carrying news and opportunities is the passage's own credit to thin ties; the dispute concerns the rare form's conditions, which job transmission does not touch.
explanations.D: This choice reports an account-management habit with no bearing on the depth or kind of ties conducted through the platform; multiple accounts are equally compatible with breadth-only use and with the claim's falsity.

questions.stem: Which of the following findings would most strengthen the fifth paragraph's claim that the friend's frankness, licensed by proven goodwill, is nearly the only correction most adults will accept?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Evidence that most adults describe themselves as open to criticism from any source, regardless of who delivers it or in what setting.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Evidence that anonymous feedback systems have become common in workplaces.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Research finding identical criticism rejected from strangers and superiors but producing durable change when delivered by a long-standing friend.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Evidence that adults with more friends report higher life satisfaction.

explanations.A: This choice records self-description, which the claim itself predicts will diverge from behavior. Professed openness to criticism from any source says nothing about which corrections adults actually accept, and the paragraph's contrast with universally available flattery suggests self-reports flatter too.
explanations.B: This choice documents the spread of a feedback mechanism, not its uptake. Anonymous systems becoming common is compatible with their corrections being routinely dismissed, which is precisely what the claim would predict for feedback unbacked by proven goodwill.
explanations.C: Correct. The claim ties acceptance of correction to the friend's frankness licensed by proven goodwill. Research holding the feedback constant while varying the source, defensive rejection from strangers, superiors, and automated assessments, durable change from the long-standing friend, with recipients citing demonstrated goodwill as what made it hearable, isolates and confirms exactly the asserted mechanism.
explanations.D: This choice links friendship quantity to life satisfaction, a general wellbeing correlation that does not address the specific claim about correction, which concerns what work the close friend's frankness uniquely accomplishes.
